methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $250.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
xAI is a Series E AI & Software company with a stated post-money valuation of $250B and $42B in cumulative capital raised. The valuation assessment triangulates across a peer set of 9 comparables—1 public (PLTR) and 8 private—all operating in AI infrastructure and software, including Series E-stage companies such as Crusoe Energy ($10.0B), Groq ($7.0B), SandboxAQ ($5.6B), Ayar Labs ($3.8B), and WEKA ($1.6B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x multiplier to the last post-money valuation of $250.0B, implying a next-stage value of $350.0B. Peer-median analysis yields a markedly different result: the median valuation across the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables is $4.7B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $10.0B. This reflects the heterogeneity in investor appetite and capability weighting across frontier AI companies.

Triangulation across these two methods produces a wide range: $4.0B (low) to $402.5B (high), with a median of $177.3B. The substantial dispersion reflects material tension between the company's last stated valuation and peer multiples. Key uncertainties include: revenue and unit economics relative to peer set, the weight assigned to founder reputation and capital intensity versus demonstrated commercial traction, and whether the $250B valuation reflects market consensus or represents aspirational pricing. Resolution of these factors is critical to narrowing the implied valuation corridor.
